name: Tommy Newsom
description: American saxophonist player in the NBC orchestra (February 25, 1929 - April 28, 2007)
abstract: Thomas Penn Newsom (February 25, 1929 – April 28, 2007) was a saxophone player in the NBC Orchestra on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson, for which he later became assistant director. Newsom was frequently the band's substitute director, whenever music director Doc Severinsen was away from the show or filling in for announcer Ed Mc…

interviews.title: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

concerts.label: The New York Pops 15th Birthday Gala Concert
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: Tommy Newsom is a great writer, writes a lot for my band and I’m so blessed to have him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sherrie Maricle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Lisa Parrott
about_quotes.evidence: I ended up doing a ton of stuff for Tommy. And he, I learnt the ethics of copying from Tommy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lisa Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 22, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: It was Zoot Sims, Tommy Newsom... Trombone were Jimmy Knepper, Willie Dennis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: And Tommy Newsom were our chief tenor players. I’m so lucky to have had that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: Me and Tommy got along like two brothers. We really did, man.
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Findley
about_quotes.evidence: And Snooky had to stay actually for a year and so did Tommy Newsom I think, a couple of guys had to stay before they moved to L.A. to finish out their contract for their buy-out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Findley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 6, 2023
